title: Jobs Filters Refactor Preservation UX Package

governingArtifacts.notesMd: Observed current UX used only as discovery and mapping input.
Desired UX is the implementation target after confirmation.
Mappings explain preservation, changes, pending decisions, and implementation gates.

rationale.summary: Jobs filters refactor work needs enough UX specificity to preserve existing behavior while allowing confirmed improvements.
rationale.whyThisProfile: The work touches existing behavior and must distinguish current, preserved, desired, and temporary refactor states.
rationale.whyThisDepth: Acceptance criteria are required so implementation can prove preserved and desired behavior before closeout.
rationale.riskNotesMd: The main risk is treating observed current behavior as desired UX without confirmation or losing behavior during refactor.

allowedOmissions.reason: Exact copy is included only where wording is governing or behavior-changing.
allowedOmissions.revisitWhen: A label, confirmation, or error message affects user understanding or tests.

allowedOmissions.reason: Pixel-level layout is not needed for this refactor preservation package.
allowedOmissions.revisitWhen: Responsive or hierarchy behavior becomes part of the refactor risk.

allowedOmissions.reason: Detailed accessibility can be added in a focused minute-detail pass unless the refactor touches keyboard, focus, or assistive behavior.
allowedOmissions.revisitWhen: The implementation changes modal focus, keyboard behavior, disabled controls, or error announcement.

blockedAreas.reason: Current-to-desired mapping marks this as pending confirmation.
blockedAreas.unblocksWhen: The user confirms whether Clear Filters should reset active browse filters.

pendingDecisions.title: Confirm exact Clear Filters confirmation copy
